title: Optimization of high volume transaction performance on a blockchain
abstract: An example operation may include one or more of identifying a newly proposed transaction of a blockchain, initiating a consensus operation to determine whether to authorize the newly proposed transaction, retrieving a key- value pair identified from a previous transaction, comparing an index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction to an index value associated with a key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction, and providing an affirmative consensus to accept the newly proposed transaction in the blockchain when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is contiguously sequential with theindex value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction.
claims:
Claims 1. A method, comprising: identifying a newly proposed transaction of a blockchain; initiating a consensus operation to determine whether to authorize the newly proposed transaction; retrieving a key-value pair identified from a previous transaction; comparing an index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction to an index value associated with a key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction; and providing an affirmative consensus to accept the newly proposed transaction in the blockchain when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: rejecting the newly proposed transaction when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is not contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 3. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining the newly proposed transaction is out-of-sequence with one or more other transactions when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is not contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the consensus operation invokes a plurality of validator node members of the blockchain, and only one validator node member of the blockchain is required to validate and append the newly proposed transaction to the blockchain. 5. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: accessing the blockchain; extracting a plurality of blockchain transactions having a first instance of a key value; and generating a compacted ledger based on the plurality of blockchain transactions, wherein the compacted ledger comprises the plurality of blockchain transactions arranged as a contiguously sequential set of key values. 6. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving a proposed key-value pair for the newly proposed transaction from a validator member of the blockchain; and accepting or rejecting the proposed key-value pair via a leader member of the blockchain based on 

 previously accepted key-value pairs. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising: assigning the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction to the newly proposed transaction via the leader member of the blockchain. 8. An apparatus, comprising: a processor configured to: identify a newly proposed transaction of a blockchain; initiate a consensus operation to determine whether to authorize the newly proposed transaction; retrieve a key-value pair identified from a previous transaction; compare an index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction to an index value associated with a key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction; and provide an affirmative consensus to accept the newly proposed transaction in the blockchain when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the processor is further configured to: reject the newly proposed transaction when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is not contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 10. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the processor is further configured to: determine the newly proposed transaction is out-of-sequence with one or more other transactions when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is not contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 11. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the consensus operation invokes a plurality of validator node members of the blockchain, and only one validator node member of the blockchain is required to validate and append the newly proposed transaction to the blockchain. 12. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the processor is further configured to: access the blockchain; extract a plurality of blockchain transactions having a first instance of a key value; and generate a compacted ledger based on the plurality of blockchain transactions, wherein the compacted ledger comprises the plurality of blockchain transactions arranged as a contiguously sequential set 

 of key values. 13. The apparatus of claim 8, further comprising: a receiver configured to receive a proposed key-value pair for the newly proposed transaction from a validator member of the blockchain; and wherein the processor is further configured to accept or reject the proposed key-value pair via a leader member of the blockchain based on previously accepted key-value pairs. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the processor is further configured to: assign the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction to the newly proposed transaction via the leader member of the blockchain. 15. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium configured to store instructions that when executed cause a processor to perform: identifying a newly proposed transaction of a blockchain; initiating a consensus operation to determine whether to authorize the newly proposed transaction; retrieving a key-value pair identified from a previous transaction; comparing an index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction to an index value associated with a key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction; and providing an affirmative consensus to accept the newly proposed transaction in the blockchain when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 16. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the processor is further configured to perform: rejecting the newly proposed transaction when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is not contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 17. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the processor is further configured to perform: determining the newly proposed transaction is out-of-sequence with one or more other transactions when the index value associated with the key-value pair of the previous transaction is not contiguously sequential with the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction. 18. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the consensus operation invokes a plurality of validator node members of the blockchain, and only one validator node member of the 

 blockchain is required to validate and append the newly proposed transaction to the blockchain. 19. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the processor is further configured to perform: accessing the blockchain; extracting a plurality of blockchain transactions having a first instance of a key value; and generating a compacted ledger based on the plurality of blockchain transactions, wherein the compacted ledger comprises the plurality of blockchain transactions arranged as a contiguously sequential set of key values. 20. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the processor is further configured to perform: receiving a proposed key-value pair for the newly proposed transaction from a validator member of the blockchain; and accepting or rejecting the proposed key-value pair via a leader member of the blockchain based on previously accepted key-value pairs; and assigning the index value associated with the key-value pair of the newly proposed transaction to the newly proposed transaction via the leader member of the blockchain.
